titleStmt.title: SII 23.431: original edition by G.V. Srinivasa Rao
No. 431. (A.R. No. 431 of 1907.) Śiṉṉamaṉūr, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District Rājasiṁhēśvara temple—on the north wall of the central shrine Māṟ. Kulaśēkhara I: year 41: 1309 A.D.

summary.p: The king is given the title ‘who was pleased to take every country’. The details of date Āṉi 14, śu. 1, Punarvasu, Monday would give the English equivalent A.D. 1309, June 9.

It is left incomplete with the mention of the fact that the Nālunagaram Padiṉeṇvishayattār, the Padineṭṭurājya-Padiṉeṇvishayattār, the brokers, the Nāṭṭuchcheṭṭis and Taḷachcheṭṭis all assembled together in the temple of Vikramapāṇḍīśvaramuḍaiya-Nāyaṉār (also mentioned in No. 430 above) for conducting some business evidently in connection with a gift to the temple of Tirupūlānduṟai-Uḍaiyār alias Rājasiṁha[chōḷī]śuram-Uḍaiyār at Arikēsarinallūr in Aḷa-nāḍu.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . tiruvāykkeḻvikkumel śrīkomāṟapaṉmarāṉa tiripuvaṉac cakkaravattikaḷ
yemmaṇṭalamuṅ koṇṭaruḷiya śrīkulajekaradevaṟku yāṇṭu 4 10 1 vatu Āṉi māta 10 4tiyati pūrvapakṣattu prathamaiyum puṇar
pūcamum peṟṟa tiṅkaḷkiḻamai nāḷ Aḷanāṭṭu Arikecari nallūr Uṭai yār tiruppūlāntuṟai (uṟai) Uṭaiyārāṉa rājasiṅhacoḷa Īśura muṭaiyā
ṟku nālunakaram patiṉeṇvi[ṣaya]ttomum patiṉeṭṭirācciyattiṟ patiṉeṇviṣayattomum tarakaromum nāṭṭuc ceṭṭikaḷomum taḷacceṭṭikaḷomum
Uṭaiyār vikramapāṇḍiśvaramuṭaiya nāyaṉār tiruma til niṟaivaṟa niṟai ntu niccayitta kāriyamāvatu Uṭaiyār tiruppūlāntu
ṟaiyuṭaiya nāyaṉār tta paṭṭiṉa pakitiyāvatuContinuation lost.
Digital edition of SII 23.431 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
